content.Text:
[Mon 2026-04-20 16:06 CDT] You are adding a "Content Engine" tab to the PostEngine React app. This is a resource hub for content creators to understand what's needed and upload content into PostEngine.

## Repo location
`/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/postengine/social-post-manager`

## What to build

### 1. Add "Content Engine" to the sidebar nav
In `client/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx`, add a new nav item:
- Title: "Content Engine"
- URL: `/content-engine`
- Icon: `BookOpen` from lucide-react
- Add it after "Calendar" in the navItems array

### 2. Create new page: `client/src/pages/content-engine.tsx`
This page is a resource hub for content creators. It should match PostEngine's existing dark theme and component style.

**Sections to include:**

#### A. Content Cadence (live from DB)
Fetch from a new API endpoint `GET /api/content-engine/cadence` (you'll create this).
Display per-dealership cadence requirements in a clean table:
- Dealership name
- Post types required (sales photos, service photos, reels, etc.)
- Posts per week / per month
- Platforms targeted

If cadence_settings table is empty, show a notice: "No cadence configured yet — set up in PostEngine Settings."

#### B. Active Shoots
Hardcoded for now (we'll make this dynamic later):
- Audi Baton Rouge — Q5 Black Optic feature content (photos + video) — Assigned: Adrian — Status: Pending
- Audi Baton Rouge — Staff intro videos for Podium — Assigned: Adrian — Status: Pending

#### C. Content Needed (per store)
Static cards showing what each store needs:
- Brian Harris BMW: Feature photos (no front plates), Staff intro videos, M/Sport lot content
- Audi Baton Rouge: Q5 Black Optic photos/video, Staff intro videos, Q7 lifestyle content
- BMW of Jackson: Dealership exterior/aerial shot, Lot lineup content, 5 Series feature content
- Harris Porsche: Dealership exterior/aerial shot, Parts/merchandise product photos

#### D. Brand Guidelines (quick reference)
4 cards, one per store:
- BHBMW: BMW Blue #1c69d4, BMW Type Next font, No front plates, No stock images
- ABR: Audi Red #BB0A21, Dark theme, No front plates, No stock images
- BMW Jackson: BMW Blue #1c69d4, BMW Type Next font, No front plates, No stock images
- Harris Porsche: Black/White/Gray, Porsche Next font, Minimalist, No front plates

#### E. Creator Roster
Simple table:
- Adrian Danylle — Video/Film — Active
- Abbie Brunet — Video/Content — Active

#### F. Brand Fonts (download links)
BMW Type Next Pro: links to https://thecooperativeagency.github.io/fonts/bmw/BMWTypeNextPro-[weight].otf for Thin, Light, Regular, Medium, Bold, Black
Audi Type: links to https://thecooperativeagency.github.io/fonts/audi/AudiType-[variant].woff2 for Normal, Bold, Italic, Wide Normal, Wide Bold, and Variable TTF

### 3. Create API endpoint in server
In `server/routes.ts`, add:

```
GET /api/content-engine/cadence
```

Query the `cadence_settings` table joined with `dealerships`:
```sql
SELECT 
  d.name as dealership_name,
  c.post_type,
  c.posts_per_day,
  c.days_of_week,
  c.platforms,
  c.is_active
FROM cadence_settings c
JOIN dealerships d ON c.dealership_id = d.id
WHERE c.is_active = 1
ORDER BY d.name, c.post_type
```

Return JSON array. If empty, return `[]`.

### 4. Add route in App.tsx
Import the new ContentEngine page and add:
```tsx
<Route path="/content-engine" component={ContentEngine} />
```

## Style requirements
- Match existing PostEngine dark theme exactly
- Use existing shadcn/ui components (Card, Table, Badge, etc.) — check what's already imported in other pages
- Use Tailwind classes consistent with the rest of the app
- Section headings should match the style of other pages

## What NOT to do
- Don't modify any existing PostEngine functionality
- Don't touch the publishing, scheduling, or queue logic
- Don't remove any existing routes or components

## After building
1. Check that the app compiles without errors: `cd /Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/postengine/social-post-manager && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -20`
2. Fix any TypeScript or import errors
3. Report what you built and any issues found
I'll start by exploring the existing codebase structure to understand the patterns, then build everything.

1. The sidebar nav structure in `client/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx` - how nav items are defined, what icons are imported
2. The routing setup in the main App component (likely `client/src/App.tsx` or similar) - how routes are defined

4. The server routes file `server/routes.ts` - how API endpoints are structured
5. What shadcn/ui components are available (check `client/src/components/ui/`)
6. The database/query pattern used (how do they query the DB in existing endpoints)

Read the full contents of the sidebar, App/routing file, server routes, and at least one example page. Be thorough - read full files, not just snippets."}

[{"type":"text","text":"Excellent! I have thoroughly explored the codebase. Let me compile a comprehensive report of all the key findings.

## PostEngine Social-Post-Manager Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. Sidebar Navigation Structure
**File:** `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/postengine/social-post-manager/client/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx`

The sidebar is built with shadcn/ui Sidebar components. Navigation items are defined in a static array:

```javascript
const navItems = [
  { title: "Dashboard", url: "/", icon: LayoutDashboard },
  { title: "Posts", url: "/posts", icon: FileText },
  { title: "New Post", url: "/posts/new", icon: PlusCircle },
  { title: "Review Queue", url: "/queue", icon: ClipboardCheck },
  { title: "Calendar", url: "/calendar", icon: CalendarDays },
  { title: "Settings", url: "/settings", icon: Settings },
];
```

Icons are imported from `lucide-react` (LayoutDashboard, FileText, PlusCircle, ClipboardCheck, CalendarDays, Settings, Building2).

The sidebar also includes:
- A branded header with PostEngine logo (custom SVG) and agency branding
- A "Filter by Store" select dropdown powered by React Query query key `["/api/dealerships"]`
- A footer showing account count and copyright
- Dynamic sidebar filter state passed down as props: `dealershipFilter` and `onDealershipFilterChange`
- Hash-based routing with `wouter/use-hash-location` hook

### 2. Routing Setup
**File:** `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/postengine/social-post-manager/client/src/App.tsx`

Routes are defined using `wouter` library with hash-based routing:

```javascript
<Route path="/" component={() => <Dashboard dealershipFilter={dealershipFilter} />} />
<Route path="/posts" component={() => <Posts dealershipFilter={dealershipFilter} />} />
<Route path="/posts/new" component={PostForm} />
<Route path="/posts/:id" component={PostForm} />
<Route path="/queue" component={() => <ReviewQueue dealershipFilter={dealershipFilter} />} />
<Route path="/calendar" component={() => <CalendarPage dealershipFilter={dealershipFilter} />} />
<Route path="/settings" component={Settings} />
<Route component={NotFound} />
```

The App structure includes:
- QueryClientProvider (React Query) wrapping entire app
- TooltipProvider for tooltips
- ThemeProvider for dark/light mode toggle in header
- SidebarProvider with custom CSS variables for sidebar width
- Layout: Sidebar (fixed left) + Main content area with header and auto-scrolling main
- Theme toggle button (Sun/Moon icons) in top-right

### 3. Example Page Component - Calendar
**File:** `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/postengine/social-post-manager/client/src/pages/calendar-page.tsx`

Pattern observed:
- Accepts `dealershipFilter` as prop from App
- Uses `useQuery` from React Query with queryKey pattern: `["/api/dealerships"]` and `["/api/posts", "calendar", dealershipFilter]`
- Fetches data using `apiRequest("GET", url)` helper function
- Uses `useMemo` for data transformation (grouping posts by day)
- Components used: Card, CardHeader, CardContent, CardTitle, Button, Badge, Skeleton
- State management: `useState` for currentDate, selectedDay
- Date handling with native JavaScript Date API
- Responsive grid layout: `grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3`
- Loading state with Skeleton components
- Empty states with icons and descriptive text
- Dealership color coding from `dealership.color` field

### 4. Server Routes API Structure
**File:** `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/postengine/social-post-manager/server/routes.ts`

RESTful API endpoints following standard patterns:

**Dealerships:**
- `GET /api/dealerships` - list all
- `GET /api/dealerships/:id` - get single
- `POST /api/dealerships` - create
- `PATCH /api/dealerships/:id` - update

**Posts:**
- `GET /api/posts` - list with optional filters (dealershipId, status, postType)
- `GET /api/p...

output.text:
Excellent! I have thoroughly explored the codebase. Let me compile a comprehensive report of all the key findings.

## PostEngine Social-Post-Manager Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. Sidebar Navigation Structure
**File:** `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/postengine/social-post-manager/client/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx`

The sidebar is built with shadcn/ui Sidebar components. Navigation items are defined in a static array:

```javascript
const navItems = [
  { title: "Dashboard", url: "/", icon: LayoutDashboard },
  { title: "Posts", url: "/posts", icon: FileText },
  { title: "New Post", url: "/posts/new", icon: PlusCircle },
  { title: "Review Queue", url: "/queue", icon: ClipboardCheck },
  { title: "Calendar", url: "/calendar", icon: CalendarDays },
  { title: "Settings", url: "/settings", icon: Settings },
];
```

Icons are imported from `lucide-react` (LayoutDashboard, FileText, PlusCircle, ClipboardCheck, CalendarDays, Settings, Building2).

The sidebar also includes:
- A branded header with PostEngine logo (custom SVG) and agency branding
- A "Filter by Store" select dropdown powered by React Query query key `["/api/dealerships"]`
- A footer showing account count and copyright
- Dynamic sidebar filter state passed down as props: `dealershipFilter` and `onDealershipFilterChange`
- Hash-based routing with `wouter/use-hash-location` hook

### 2. Routing Setup
**File:** `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/postengine/social-post-manager/client/src/App.tsx`

Routes are defined using `wouter` library with hash-based routing:

```javascript
<Route path="/" component={() => <Dashboard dealershipFilter={dealershipFilter} />} />
<Route path="/posts" component={() => <Posts dealershipFilter={dealershipFilter} />} />
<Route path="/posts/new" component={PostForm} />
<Route path="/posts/:id" component={PostForm} />
<Route path="/queue" component={() => <ReviewQueue dealershipFilter={dealershipFilter} />} />
<Route path="/calendar" component={() => <CalendarPage dealershipFilter={dealershipFilter} />} />
<Route path="/settings" component={Settings} />
<Route component={NotFound} />
```

The App structure includes:
- QueryClientProvider (React Query) wrapping entire app
- TooltipProvider for tooltips
- ThemeProvider for dark/light mode toggle in header
- SidebarProvider with custom CSS variables for sidebar width
- Layout: Sidebar (fixed left) + Main content area with header and auto-scrolling main
- Theme toggle button (Sun/Moon icons) in top-right

### 3. Example Page Component - Calendar
**File:** `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/postengine/social-post-manager/client/src/pages/calendar-page.tsx`

Pattern observed:
- Accepts `dealershipFilter` as prop from App
- Uses `useQuery` from React Query with queryKey pattern: `["/api/dealerships"]` and `["/api/posts", "calendar", dealershipFilter]`
- Fetches data using `apiRequest("GET", url)` helper function
- Uses `useMemo` for data transformation (grouping posts by day)
- Components used: Card, CardHeader, CardContent, CardTitle, Button, Badge, Skeleton
- State management: `useState` for currentDate, selectedDay
- Date handling with native JavaScript Date API
- Responsive grid layout: `grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3`
- Loading state with Skeleton components
- Empty states with icons and descriptive text
- Dealership color coding from `dealership.color` field

### 4. Server Routes API Structure
**File:** `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/postengine/social-post-manager/server/routes.ts`

RESTful API endpoints following standard patterns:

**Dealerships:**
- `GET /api/dealerships` - list all
- `GET /api/dealerships/:id` - get single
- `POST /api/dealerships` - create
- `PATCH /api/dealerships/:id` - update

**Posts:**
- `GET /api/posts` - list with optional filters (dealershipId, status, postType)
- `GET /api/posts/stats` - aggregated stats
- `GET /api/posts/:id` - get single
- `POST /api/posts` - create
- `PATCH /api/posts/:id` - update (with auto-archiving logic)
- `DELETE /api/posts/:id` - delete
- `POST /api/posts/bulk-approve` - approve multiple posts
- `POST /api/posts/:id/publish` - manually publish
- `POST /api/posts/:id/notify` - send approval request

**Integration endpoints:**
- `POST /api/telegram/webhook` - Telegram bot approval/rejection callbacks
- `POST /api/drive/scan` - scan Google Drive folders for new posts
- `POST /api/notify/weekly` - send weekly summary

**Cadence Settings (posting schedule automation):**
- `GET /api/cadence` - list settings
- `POST /api/cadence` - create
- `PATCH /api/cadence/:id` - update
- `DELETE /api/cadence/:id` - delete

**Activity:**
- `GET /api/activity` - activity log
- `GET /api/dashboard` - comprehensive dashboard stats

Database queries via `storage` object:
- All routes call `storage.getDealerships()`, `storage.getPosts()`, `storage.createPost()`, etc.
- Error handling with try-catch and Zod schema validation
- Activity logging on state changes (created, scheduled, published, rejected, deleted)

### 5. Available shadcn/ui Components
**Path:** `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/postengine/social-post-manager/client/src/components/ui/`

Complete list of 46+ shadcn/ui components available:
- Layout: sidebar, accordion, collapsible, drawer, sheet, navigation-menu, breadcrumb, separator
- Form: form, input, textarea, label, select, checkbox, radio-group, switch, toggle, toggle-group, slider
- Data display: table, pagination, progress, carousel, chart, avatar, badge, alert
- Interactions: button, dropdown-menu, context-menu, dialog, alert-dialog, popover, hover-card, command, menubar
- Presentation: card, scroll-area, tabs, resizable, tooltip
- Special: input-otp, aspect-ratio, skeleton, toast/toaster

### 6. Database & Query Pattern
**Files:** 
- `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/postengine/social-post-manager/server/storage.ts`
- `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/postengine/social-post-manager/shared/schema.ts`

**Database Layer:**
- Uses `better-sqlite3` with Drizzle ORM
- SQLite database file: `sqlite.db`
- Tables created in constructor if not exist

**Schema Definitions (Drizzle + Zod):**
1. **Dealerships** - stores managed accounts (id, name, brand, domain, location, social handles, caption template, color)
2. **Posts** - social media content (id, dealership_id, status, post_type, vehicle_info, caption, hashtags, cta_block, media_urls, platforms, scheduled_for, published_at, folder_source, created_at)
3. **Cadence Settings** - automation rules (dealership_id, post_type, days_of_week, posts_per_day, auto_time, platforms, is_active)
4. **Activity Log** - audit trail (post_id, dealership_id, action, details, created_at)

**Query Pattern in Storage Class:**
```javascript
// Simple reads
getDealerships(): Dealership[] {
  return db.select().from(dealerships).all();
}

// Filtered queries
getPosts(filters?: { dealershipId?: number; status?: string; postType?: string }): Post[] {
  let query = db.select().from(posts);
  const conditions = [];
  if (filters?.dealershipId) conditions.push(eq(posts.dealershipId, filters.dealershipId));
  if (filters?.status) conditions.push(eq(posts.status, filters.status));
  if (filters?.postType) conditions.push(eq(posts.postType, filters.postType));
  
  if (conditions.length > 0) {
    return query.where(and(...conditions)).orderBy(desc(posts.createdAt)).all();
  }
  return query.orderBy(desc(posts.createdAt)).all();
}

// CRUD operations
createPost(data: InsertPost): Post {
  return db.insert(posts).values({
    ...data,
    createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
  }).returning().get();
}

updatePost(id: number, data: Partial<InsertPost>): Post | undefined {
  return db.update(posts).set(data).where(eq(posts.id, id)).returning().get();
}
```

Zod schemas used for validation:
- `insertDealershipSchema` and `insertPostSchema` validate request bodies
- Drizzle `createInsertSchema` generates Zod schemas from table definitions
- Routes use `schema.parse(req.body)` to validate

**Frontend Query Client Pattern:**
```javascript
// API request helper
export async function apiRequest(method: string, url: string, data?: unknown): Promise<Response>

// React Query usage
const { data: posts, isLoading } = useQuery<Post[]>({
  queryKey: ["/api/posts", queryParams.toString()],
  queryFn: async () => {
    const res = await apiRequest("GET", `/api/posts?${queryParams.toString()}`);
    return res.json();
  },
});

// Mutations for mutations
const createMutation = useMutation({
  mutationFn: async (data: FormValues) => {
    const res = await apiRequest("POST", "/api/posts", payload);
    return res.json();
  },
  onSuccess: () => {
    queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ["/api/posts"] });
    toast({ title: "Post created" });
  },
});
```

### Key Architecture Insights

- **State Management:** React Query for server state, useState for local UI state
- **Forms:** React Hook Form + Zod for validation (PostForm component)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS with shadcn/ui components
- **Icons:** lucide-react for all UI icons
- **Data Flow:** Client -> API -> Storage (Database)
- **Notifications:** Toast notifications via custom hook + Telegram webhooks for approvals
- **Features:** Multi-dealership support, post scheduling, approval workflow, activity audit trail, Google Drive integration

content.Text:
1	import { sqliteTable, text, integer } from "drizzle-orm/sqlite-core";
2	import { createInsertSchema } from "drizzle-zod";
3	import { z } from "zod";
4	
5	// Dealerships
6	export const dealerships = sqliteTable("dealerships", {
7	  id: integer("id").primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
8	  name: text("name").notNull(),
9	  brand: text("brand").notNull(), // BMW, Audi, Porsche
10	  domain: text("domain").notNull(),
11	  location: text("location").notNull(),
12	  instagramHandle: text("instagram_handle"),
13	  facebookPage: text("facebook_page"),
14	  tiktokHandle: text("tiktok_handle"),
15	  captionTemplate: text("caption_template"), // AutoCaption-style rules as text
16	  color: text("color").notNull().default("#01696F"), // brand accent color
17	});
18	
19	export const insertDealershipSchema = createInsertSchema(dealerships).omit({ id: true });
20	export type InsertDealership = z.infer<typeof insertDealershipSchema>;
21	export type Dealership = typeof dealerships.$inferSelect;
22	
23	// Social Posts
24	export const posts = sqliteTable("posts", {
25	  id: integer("id").primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
26	  dealershipId: integer("dealership_id").notNull(),
27	  status: text("status").notNull().default("draft"), // draft, queued, scheduled, published, rejected
28	  postType: text("post_type").notNull().default("inventory"), // inventory, promo, lifestyle, announcement
29	  vehicleInfo: text("vehicle_info"), // e.g. "2026 BMW X5 M60"
30	  caption: text("caption"), // Instagram/Facebook caption
31	  captionFacebook: text("caption_facebook"), // Facebook caption (same as IG unless different)
32	  captionGmb: text("caption_gmb"), // Google My Business caption
33	  hashtags: text("hashtags"),
34	  ctaBlock: text("cta_block"), // dealership-specific CTA
35	  mediaUrls: text("media_urls"), // JSON array of URLs
36	  mediaType: text("media_type").default("image"), // image, video, carousel
37	  platforms: text("platforms").default('["instagram","facebook"]'), // JSON array
38	  scheduledFor: text("scheduled_for"), // ISO datetime
39	  publishedAt: text("published_at"),
40	  folderSource: text("folder_source"), // Drive folder path
41	  notes: text("notes"),
42	  createdAt: text("created_at").notNull().$defaultFn(() => new Date().toISOString()),
43	});
44	
45	export const insertPostSchema = createInsertSchema(posts).omit({ id: true, createdAt: true });
46	export type InsertPost = z.infer<typeof insertPostSchema>;
47	export type Post = typeof posts.$inferSelect;
48	
49	// Post Cadence Settings
50	export const cadenceSettings = sqliteTable("cadence_settings", {
51	  id: integer("id").primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
52	  dealershipId: integer("dealership_id").notNull(),
53	  postType: text("post_type").notNull(), // New Cars, Pre-Owned Cars, Service, Parts & Accessories
54	  daysOfWeek: text("days_of_week").notNull().default('[]'), // JSON array: ["monday","wednesday","friday"]
55	  postsPerDay: integer("posts_per_day").notNull().default(1),
56	  autoTime: integer("auto_time", { mode: "boolean" }).notNull().default(true), // let app pick best time
57	  manualTime: text("manual_time"), // e.g. "10:00" if autoTime is false
58	  platforms: text("platforms").notNull().default('["instagram","facebook","googlebusiness"]'), // JSON array
59	  isActive: integer("is_active", { mode: "boolean" }).notNull().default(true),
60	  createdAt: text("created_at").notNull().$defaultFn(() => new Date().toISOString()),
61	  updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull().$defaultFn(() => new Date().toISOString()),
62	});
63	
64	export const insertCadenceSchema = createInsertSchema(cadenceSettings).omit({ id: true, createdAt: true, updatedAt: true });
65	export type InsertCadence = z.infer<typeof insertCadenceSchema>;
66	export type CadenceSetting = typeof cadenceSettings.$inferSelect;
67	
68	// Activity log
69	export const activityLog = sqliteTable("activity_log", {
70	  id: integer("id").primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
71	  postId: integer("post_id")...
